title: The casualties of Iraqi missile attacks on Tehran, Iran in the last 52 days of the war
pages_or_sections: abstract / summary lines 52-59 in tool view
strengths: Dedicated study of Tehran casualties using data from Army Staff Headquarters daily reports; gives direct city-specific civilian fatality and injury counts.
limitations: Accessible abstract text does not itself display the exact start and end dates in the tool; the date window comes from a search snippet tied to the same article.
notes: Core source for the Tehran civilian numerator.

citation_short: Search result snippet for Rafati article
title: Search snippet for the Tehran casualty article
author_or_org: Search engine snippet tied to ScienceDirect article
strengths: Provides the exact 52-day dates for the same Tehran casualty article.
limitations: Snippet-level evidence rather than a full page line from the article itself.
notes: Used only to lock the exact episode dates; flagged as a weaker support type.

title: Ballistic missile attacks on Iranian cities during the Iran-Iraq war
strengths: Corroborates the broader missile campaign and confirms the use of Army Staff Headquarters daily reports as data source logic.

title: The Comprehensive Transportation and Traffic Studies of Tehran Metropolitan Area in the Islamic Republic of Iran
pages_or_sections: p. 118 and p. 125 in tool view
strengths: Provides Tehran population of 6,042,584 in 1986, the best city-specific stock recovered near the 1988 episode.
limitations: Population table is not a wartime daily stock and predates the missile wave by two years.
notes: Used as the city-population baseline for the civilian denominator.

title: Iraq Missile Overview
pages_or_sections: main text; line 92 in tool view
strengths: Provides a high-end displacement claim that more than two million people fled Tehran during the seven-week missile exchange.
limitations: Secondary analytical source and not a daily population series.
notes: Used only as the conservative lower-bound adjustment for exposed civilian stock.

title: Iran: Back to 1987?
pages_or_sections: main text; lines 380-381 in tool view
strengths: Corroborates the 52-day, 118-missile, 422-killed, 1,579-injured Tehran episode and notes that nearly a quarter of residents fled.
limitations: Analytical retrospective, not a contemporaneous administrative source.
notes: Used to cross-check the displacement narrative and casualty totals.
